wikidata_candidates.label: Ronald H. Miller
wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The two records describe individuals with the same common name but completely different professional backgrounds and locations. The card record describes a mechanical designer of industrial vehicles in Ontario, Canada, while the Wikidata entity refers to a religious studies scholar and academic in the United States.

results.possibleIdentity: Ronald H. Miller was a mechanical engineer and designer based in Woodstock, Ontario, Canada, during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by Timberjack Machines Limited (later a division of Eaton Yale & Towne), a prominent manufacturer of logging equipment such as skidders and harvesters. Miller is recorded as a technical participant in the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) matching service, an organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to facilitate collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. His professional profile, as documented in technical registration records from the period, identified him as a designer with expertise in mechanical engineering, specifically within the industrial machinery sector of Woodstock.
